name: A curated list of Domain-Driven Design (DDD), Command Query Responsibility Segregation (CQRS), Event Sourcing, and Event Storming resources
description: A curated list of Domain-Driven Design (DDD), Command Query Responsibility Segregation (CQRS), Event Sourcing, and Event Storming resources

links.title: Applying Domain Driven Design with CQRS and Event Sourcing
links.description: A walkthrough of using EventStorming and other modeling techniques to build a CQRS and Event Sourcing-based prototype for a fictional business domain.

links.title: Architecture Modernization
links.description: Concrete tools, techniques, and processes to align software architecture with your business domains, organizational design, team topologies, and corporate strategy.

links.title: Collaborative Software Design
links.description: A practical guide for effectively involving all stakeholders in the design of software.A practical guide for effectively involving all stakeholders in the design of software.

links.title: CQRS
links.description: Notes by Mark Nijhof from his experiences learning DDD and CQRS from Greg Young.  There is an extensive sample project that accompanies this book.

links.title: Domain-Driven Design: Tackling the Complexity at the Heart of Software
links.description: The canonical book that coined the term Domain-Driven Design.  Also known as the "Blue Book."

links.title: Domain-Driven Design Distilled
links.description: Very good starter book before you read [Implementing Domain-Driven Design](https://vaughnvernon.co/?page_id=168#iddd) or [Domain-Driven Design: Tackling the Complexity at the Heart of Software](https://amzn.com/0321125215).

links.title: Domain-Driven Design in PHP
links.description: Real examples written in PHP showcasing DDD Architectural Styles, Tactical Design, and Bounded Context Integration.

links.title: Domain-Driven Rails
links.description: Domain-Driven Rails describes 11 techniques (from Bounded Contexts to Event Sourcing) that you can use separately and together in new and old Rails apps to achieve better architecture.

links.title: Domain Modeling Made Functional
links.description: Tackle Software Complexity with Domain-Driven Design and F#.

links.title: Domain Specific Languages
links.description: Tangentially connected to DDD, describes the importance of the Ubiquitous Language and working together with domain experts, along with lots of technical details.

links.title: Event Sourcing and CQRS with .NET Core and SQL Server
links.description: A walkthrough of using Domain-Driven Design with Event Sourcing and CQRS using ASP.NET Core and SQL Server in production.

links.title: Hands-On Domain-Driven Design with .NET Core
links.description: Tackling complexity in the heart of software by putting DDD principles into practice by Alexey Zimarev.

links.title: Implementing Domain-Driven Design
links.description: Also a canonical book presenting a top-down approach to understanding Domain-Driven Design.  Also known as the "Red Book."

links.title: Implementing DDD, CQRS and Event Sourcing
links.description: Learn how to implement DDD, CQRS and Event Sourcing. Understand the theory and put it into practice with JavaScript and Node.js.

links.title: Introducing Event Storming: An act of Deliberate Collective Learning
links.description: The deepest tutorial and explanation about EventStorming, straight from the inventor.

links.title: Learning Domain-Driven Design: Aligning Software Architecture and Business Strategy
links.description: Learn the essential patterns and practices of domain-driven design and how to apply them in your day-to-day work, both in greenfield and brownfield projects.

links.title: Microsoft .NET - Architecting Applications for the Enterprise (2nd Edition)
links.description: A software architect’s digest of core practices, pragmatically applied.

links.title: Patterns, Principles, and Practices of Domain-Driven Design (1st Edition)
links.description: Methods for managing complex software construction following the practices, principles and patterns of Domain-Driven Design with code examples in C#.

links.title: Secure by Design
links.description: Shows how to use Domain-Driven Design to avoid security vulnerabilities.

links.title: Specification by Example
links.description: This book distills from the experience of leading teams worldwide effective ways to specify, test, and deliver software in short, iterative delivery cycles. Case studies in this book range from small web startups to large financial institutions, working in many processes including XP, Scrum, and Kanban.

links.title: Versioning in an Event Sourced System
links.description: Have you had troubles with versioning an Event Sourced system? Just getting into a new Event Sourced system for the first time? This book is the definitive guide of how to handle versioning over long periods of time.

links.title: What is Domain-Driven Design?
links.description: A quick read exploring the core patterns and principles of Domain-Driven Design, as well as strategies for applying DDD in brownfield projects.

links.title: Writing Great Specifications
links.description: Writing Great Specifications is an example-rich tutorial that teaches you how to write good Gherkin specification documents that take advantage of Specification by Example's benefits.

links.title: The Anatomy of Domain-Driven Design - The Infographic
links.description: An infographic on the anatomy of Domain-Driven Design.

links.title: Architecting Modern Web Applications with ASP.NET Core and Microsoft Azure
links.description: Provides end-to-end guidance on building monolithic web applications using DDD, ASP.NET Core, and Azure. [eShopOnWeb sample](https://github.com/dotnet-architecture/eShopOnWeb)

links.title: Architecture Patterns with Python
links.description: A book about Pythonic application architecture patterns for managing complexity.

links.title: CQRS Journey
links.description: Exploring CQRS and Event Sourcing.

links.title: Domain-Driven Design: The First 15 Years
links.description: To celebrate the anniversary, we've asked prominent authors in the software design world to contribute old and new essays. With contributions by Martin Fowler, James Coplien, Rebecca Wirfs-Brock, Mel Conway, and many more.

links.title: DDD Reference
links.description: A summary of the patterns and definitions of DDD.

links.title: Domain Driven Design Quickly
links.description: Domain Driven Design Quickly is a short, quick-readable summary and introduction to the fundamentals of DDD.

links.title: Effective Aggregate Design
links.description: In this three-part series, Vaughn Vernon walks through some common design pitfalls, discusses the pros and cons of various aggregate modeling choices, and provides rules of thumb to guide modeling of aggregates.

links.title: Getting Started with DDD when Surrounded by Legacy Systems
links.description: Describes three strategies for getting started with DDD when you have a big commitment to legacy systems.

links.title: Living Documentation by Design, with Domain-Driven Design
links.description: Discover how a Living Documentation can help you in all aspects of your projects, from the business goals to the business domain knowledge, architecture and design, processes and deployment, even if you hate writing documentation.

links.title: .NET Microservices: Architecture for Containerized .NET Applications
links.description: An introduction to developing microservices-based applications and managing them using containers. [eShopOnContainers sample](https://github.com/dotnet-architecture/eShopOnContainers)

links.title: The Semantic JavaScript Backend for Event-Driven Development
links.description: A 68 pages long free ebook on what DDD, CQRS and event-sourcing are, and how they fit each other.

links.title: DDD Magazine from Xebia #1
links.description: This magazine is packed with visionary and practical insights, based on personal, business, and customer experiences to support you on your DDD journey

links.title: Visual Collaboration Tools
links.description: Visual Collaboration Tools is a book for teams building software. It describes tools that help us in our daily job, and also present field stories from different practitioners.

links.title: Advanced Distributed Systems Design
links.description: Online training course from Udi Dahan.  Udi's live training schedule can be found [here](http://udidahan.com/training/).

links.title: DDD Workshops across Europe and Online
links.description: Public Workshops by Eric Evans, Alberto Brandolini, Mathias Verraes, Rebecca Wirfs-Brock, Nick Tune, and many more.

links.title: DDD Workshop by Xebia
links.description: public and in-house Domain-driven design workshops from foundation to professional level.

links.title: Distilling Domain-Driven Design
links.description: Vaughn Vernon's online training course.

links.title: Domain Language eLearning
links.description: Using our video lessons with Eric Evans, author of the original book on Domain-Driven Design (DDD), teach yourself techniques for evolving practical models that improve your software — not just your documents.

links.title: Event Sourcery
links.description: Introduction to DDD, CQRS, and Event Sourcing.

links.title: Greg Young's CQRS Class
links.description: These videos include the entirety of Greg Young's DDD, CQRS, and Event Sourcing class.

links.title: Mixter
links.description: CQRS and Event Sourcing Koans.

links.title: Nomad PHP
links.description: Introduction to Event Sourcing and CQRS.

links.title: Domain-Driven Design Fundamentals
links.description: Teaches the fundamentals of Domain-Driven Design (DDD) through a demonstration of customer interactions and a complex demo application, along with advice from Eric Evans.

links.title: Domain-Driven Design in Practice
links.description: A descriptive, in-depth walk-through for applying Domain-Driven Design principles in practice.

links.title: Modern Software Architecture: Domain Models, CQRS, and Event Sourcing
links.description: This course covers DDD analysis patterns helpful in discovering the top-level architecture of a business domain. Architects and developers will find details of implementation patterns such as Domain Model, CQRS, and Event Sourcing.

links.title: Rails + Domain Driven Design Workshop
links.description: Introduction to DDD, fundamentals of CQRS & Event Sourcing for Ruby/Rails developers.

links.title: Reactive Architecture: CQRS and Event Sourcing
links.description: This course will discuss the reasons we use CQRS/ES, what benefits it provides us, but also what it will cost us to use. We will see how CQRS/ES can impact the scalability, consistency, and availability of our application.

links.title: Reactive Architecture: Domain Driven Design
links.description: Use DDD to decompose a problem domain into manageable parts. Learn how those parts can become the foundation of Reactive Microservices and Reactive Architecture. Free, online, self-paced. Certificate offered upon completion.

links.title: Spatie's Event Sourcing Course
links.description: Learn how to build event sourced applications in Laravel using Spatie's event-sourcing package. Has 2 hours of video content and an accompanying ebook.

links.title: DDD Europe
links.description: Recordings of talks given at DDD Europe.

links.title: SkillsCasts by SkillsMatter
links.description: Searching DDD returns various talks given by Greg Young, Alberto Brandolini, and Dan North, etc.

Curator of [Visual Collaboration Tools](https://leanpub.com/visualcollaborationtools/) and host of the [Software Crafts Podcast](https://www.softwarecraftspodcast.com/)

links.title: GlobalAppTesting engineering
links.description: GlobalAppTesting's engineering blog with materials on practical DDD and CQRS.

links.title: BDD in GO
links.description: Sample app demonstrating the use of Cucumber + GO for a BDD testing approach. Blog post can be found [here](https://dev.to/jankaritech/demonstrating-bdd-behavior-driven-development-in-go-1eci).

links.title: Citerus DDD Sample App GO Port
links.description: This is an attempt to port the [DDD Sample App](https://github.com/citerus/dddsample-core) to idiomatic Go. It can be run in a dockerized mode for previewing the application.

links.title: DDD by Refactoring
links.description: Complete serverless application to show how to apply DDD, Clean Architecture, and CQRS by practical refactoring of a Go project. A full blog series about it can be found at <https://threedots.tech/>.

links.title: DDD Food App
links.description: Sample DDD application implementing the 4 layers (Domain, Infrastructure, Application and Interface) and considering two domain patterns. There's a blog article written for it [here](https://dev.to/stevensunflash/using-domain-driven-design-ddd-in-golang-3ee5).

links.title: DDD Sample in GO
links.description: Just another sample application implementing the four layers of DDD.

links.title: Evolutive CRUD API
links.description: API implementation with full CRUD using a SOLID, Hexagonal Architecture. There is a series of blog post written for it at <https://blog.friendsofgo.tech/>.

links.title: Simple Hexagonal Architecture PoC API
links.description: PoC for a patients API using the hexagonal architecture pattern.

links.title: Azure DDD boilerplate
links.description: A boilerplate project for DDD in Azure using a custom handler and Cosmos DB for event sourcing

links.title: Better code with DDD building blocks
links.description: solution presents usage of DDD tactical patterns to achieve better readability and expressiveness of the code. Applying DDD patterns together with ubiquitous language closes the gap between language spoken by experts and the team and language used in the code.

links.title: CQRS-DDD Example
links.description: Domain Driven Design, CQRS, & Event Sourcing Example using GetEventStore, CommonDomain, NServiceBus, Entity Framework, SQL Server, SignalR.

links.title: Companion Code for Microsoft .NET Architecting Applications for the Enterprise
links.description: An event based Micro ERP.

links.title: ContosoUniversityCore
links.description: ContosoUniversity on ASP.NET Core with Full .NET Framework.

links.title: DDD-starter-dotnet
links.description: Sample implementation and comparison of various approaches to building DDD applications. Useful as a baseline to quickly start a DDD .net project.

links.title: DDDInventoryItemFSharp
links.description: An idiomatic F# implementation of Domain-Driven Design

links.title: DotNet CQRS Intro
links.description: Examples of implementation CQRS with Event Sourcing - evolutionary approach (no CQRS, separate models and commands with the same model, separate models and commands with separate models, separate storage engines, event sourcing).

links.title: EISK
links.description: .NET CLI and VS Templates with simple use cases to build scalable applications on top of .net core with architectural best practices (DDD, onion architecture etc).

links.title: EmailMaker
links.description: Email marketing ASP.NET Core MVC and ASP.NET MVC demo app demonstrating [CoreDdd](https://github.com/xhafan/coreddd) usage

links.title: Equinox Project
links.description: Full ASP.NET Core 3.1 application with Clean Architecture, DDD, CQRS and Event Sourcing concepts

links.description: Full ASP.NET Core 3.1 reference application from Microsoft showing monolithic deployment architecture

links.title: eShopOnContainersDDD
links.description: eShop fullstack example featuring catalog, basket, checkout, and order bounded contexts

links.title: Example of Domain-Driven Design in F#
links.description: Example of Domain Driven Design for the game of checkers. There are two files: a scratch file with a series of designs, and a final version.

links.description: These are the sample Bounded Contexts for C#.NET from the book "Implementing Domain-Driven Design" by Vaughn Vernon.

links.description: Starter template for a modular application in Clean Architecture DDD style with synchronous interaction between modules within single transaction. Perfect for ERP applications.

links.description: Reactive Trader Cloud by Adaptive Consulting.

links.description: Greg Young's SimpleCQRS in F#.

links.description: Greg Young's "Simplest Thing" CQRS with Event Sourcing project.

links.description: Implementation of basic Todo app via tastejs/todomvc in C#/Typescript with eventsourcing, cqrs, and domain driven design

links.title: Eventuria gsd
links.description: Haskell todo list reactive application with DDD, CQRS and Event Sourcing, FRP.

links.description: DDD-CQRS sample v2.0 project that helps you with starting out advanced domain modeling using Spring, JPA and testing.

links.description: A pragmatic application of Event Sourcing and CQRS in Java with good references for common related problems, e.g. event ordering and idempotency.

links.description: Examples of implementation CQRS with Event Sourcing - evolutionary approach (no CQRS, separate models and commands with the same model, separate models and commands with separate models, separate storage engines, event sourcing).

links.title: EventStormingWorkshop - Designing Cloud Native Microservices On AWS
links.description: A concrete sample to go through EventStorming workshop and implement DDD tactical design pattern in Java, apply AWS cloud native services to build up business Event based Coffeeshop scenario.

links.title: DDDSample
links.description: Sample DDD project using Spring Boot (originally hosted in http://dddsample.sourceforge.net/)

links.title: Eclipse CargoTracker
links.description: This project demonstrates how you can develop applications with the Jakarta EE platform using widely adopted architectural best practices like Domain-Driven Design (DDD). [The code](https://github.com/eclipse-ee4j/cargotracker/) is intended to mirror a non-trivial application that developers in the real work would work on. It attempts to demonstrate first-hand how you can use Jakarta EE to effectively meet practical enterprise concerns such as productivity, agility, testability, flexibility, maintainability, scalability and security. The project is directly based on the well known original [Java DDD sample application](https://github.com/citerus/dddsample-core) developed by DDD pioneer Eric Evans' company Domain Language and the Swedish software consulting company Citerus.

links.title: https://github.com/felipexw/clean-arch-ddd-intro
links.description: Simple DDD + Clean Architecture using Micronaut.

links.title: DDD CQRS Todo Sample
links.description: DDD CQRS ADR hexagonal architecture implementation built with PHP 7 and Symfony 5.

links.title: DDD Modulith
links.description: A DDD Onion Architecture implementation with Symfony 7 as modulith.

links.title: DDD Wish List
links.description: A sample application in PHP built with Symfony 3 and Vue.js.

links.title: DDD Playground
links.description: Sample implementation in PHP.

links.title: Eric Evans DDD Cargo Sample
links.description: PHP 7 Version of the cargo sample used in Eric Evans DDD book

links.title: Shop Cart in PHP
links.description: Sample project that demonstrates how simple shop cart can look like. Domain objects,Doctrine integration.,TDD,layers,unit testing

links.description: Lightweight infrastructure for doing eventsourcing using aggregates.

links.title: Akka.NET
links.description: Akka.NET is a toolkit and runtime for building highly concurrent, distributed, and fault tolerant event-driven applications on .NET & Mono.

links.title: ABP
links.description: Successor of ASP.NET Boilerplate - ASP.NET Core based application framework to create NLayered, Domain Driven Designed web applications with a microservice focused modular architecture

links.title: ASP.NET Boilerplate
links.description: ASP.NET MVC, Web API and ASP.NET Core based application framework to create NLayered, Domain Driven Designed web Applications implementing best practices.

links.title: ByValue
links.description: This library helps to create ValueObjects (even with collection properties) with properly implemented equality behavior.

links.title: CoreDdd
links.description: Set of open-source .NET libraries helping with DDD and CQRS, with NHibernate persistence

links.title: Dolittle
links.description: Build better applications with Dolittle. An Event Sourced, Microservice platform oriented around DDD with developer productivity and simplicity front and center.

links.title: Edument CQRS and Intentful BDD Testing Starter Kit
links.description: Library and tutorial for how to build CQRS/ES applications, including a BDD style testing framework.

links.title: EventFlow
links.description: Async/await first CQRS+ES and DDD framework for .NET http://geteventflow.net/.

links.title: Core.EventStore
links.description: A library to facilitate communication between CommandService and QueryService. The Idea is when any event occures in commandService, it should be persisted in QueryService in MongoDb.

links.description: A set of libraries for CQRS and Event Sourcing, with a Domain-Driven Design flavor.

links.description: Postgresql as a Document Database and Event Store for .Net Applications.

links.title: MassTransit
links.description: Distributed Application Framework for .NET.

links.title: MediatR
links.description: Supports request/response, commands, queries, notifications and events, synchronous and async with intelligent dispatching via C# generic variance.

links.title: MessageRouter
links.description: Described in this video: [The Beating Heart of CQRS, or Actor-Based Message Routing on the CLR](https://vimeo.com/171178586) by Paulmichael Blasucci at the New York F# .NET User Group.

links.title: NetDevPack
links.description: A smart set of common classes and implementations to improve your development productivity using .NET (DDD, CQRS, Specification Pattern, MediatR, Validations, Notifications).

links.title: NEventStore
links.description: A persistence library used to abstract different storage implementations when using event sourcing as storage mechanism.

links.title: NServiceBus
links.description: Service bus for .NET.

links.title: Projac
links.description: Projac is a set of projection libraries that allow you to write projections targetting various backing stores.

links.title: shriek-fx
links.description: An simple,elegant and useful Domain-Driven Design and CQRS framework developed using .NET Core 2.0.

links.title: SqlStreamStore
links.description: .NET Stream Store library targeting SQL based implementations.

links.title: Streamstone
links.description: Event Store for Azure Table Storage.

links.title: Stringly.Typed
links.description: Making it easier to convert strings to/from .NET types.

links.title: Xer.Cqrs
links.description: A simple library for creating applications based on the CQRS pattern with support for attribute routing and hosted handlers. Developed in C# targeting .NET Standard 1.0.

links.title: Deveel Repository
links.description: A simple implementation of the Repository pattern for .NET, supporting MongoDB and Entity Framework, extending the model with further utilities (caching, paging, validation, etc.).

links.title: Event Store
links.description: The open-source, functional database with Complex Event Processing in JavaScript.

links.title: Eventsourcing
links.description: Business event capture and querying framework.

links.title: Message DB
links.description: Microservice Native Event Store and Message Store for Postgres. A fully-featured event store and message store implemented in PostgreSQL for Pub/Sub, Event Sourcing, Messaging, and Evented Microservices applications.

links.title: Serialized
links.description: Complete platform for Event Sourcing & CQRS.

links.title: Event Bus
links.description: Traceable, extendable and minimalist event bus implementation for Elixir with built-in event store and event watcher based on ETS.

links.title: cqrs.js
links.description: CQRS implementations in node.js.  Includes [node-eventstore](https://github.com/adrai/node-eventstore), [node-cqrs-domain](https://github.com/adrai/node-cqrs-domain), [node-eventdenormalizer](https://github.com/adrai/node-cqrs-eventdenormalizer), [node-cqrs-saga](https://github.com/adrai/node-cqrs-saga).

links.title: Bitloops Language (BL)
links.description: Open-source, 4th-generation, transpiled programming language that helps you write clean code, well-designed systems, and build high-quality software that is testable, auditable and maintainable using DDD and Hexagonal Architecture.

links.title: Node API Boilerplate
links.description: NodeJS web API boilerplate for DDD and Clean Architecture applications.

links.title: akka-ddd
links.description: Reusable artifacts for building applications on top of the Akka platform following CQRS/DDDD-based approach.

links.title: Apache Isis
links.description: Apache Isis is a framework for rapidly developing domain-driven apps in Java.

links.title: Axon Framework
links.description: The axon framework is focused on making life easier for developers that want to create a java application based on the CQRS principles.

links.title: DDDplus framework
links.description: A lightweight flexible development framework for complex business architecture based on DDD.

links.title: JESA
links.description: Event sourced aggregates for Java.

links.title: Lagom
links.description: The Lagom Framework is a microservices framework for the Java Virtual Machine, with APIs for the Java and Scala languages. It includes an Event Sourcing/CQRS based persistence module.

links.title: SeedStack's Business Framework
links.description: A set of building blocks that enable you to code business logic according to the Domain-Driven Design (DDD) approach.

links.title: Spine Event Engine
links.description: a CQRS/ES framework for building cloud applications. Defines Bounded Contexts with their Commands, Events, and Entity states in Protobuf. The backend logic is written in Java, on top of the Proto-generated code. Client code in Java, JS or Dart communicates with the backend via gRPC.

links.title: Broadway
links.description: Broadway is a (PHP) project providing infrastructure and testing helpers for creating CQRS and event sourced applications.

links.title: CodefyPHP Framework
links.description: A PHP framework for codefying and building complex applications using Domain-Driven Design, CQRS, and Event Sourcing.

links.title: Ecotone
links.description: Enables message driven architecture in PHP and provides building blocks to follow DDD and CQRS principles.

links.title: PHP Glossary
links.description: Apply a Term Analysis to extract domain terms vs out-of-scope terms in a DDD manner.

links.title: Eventsoucing in Python
links.description: Mature, stable Python library for event sourcing and DDD. Supports wide variety of databases, different kinds of orderings of domain events, application level encryption, snapshotting, optimistic concurrency control, and process events. Applications, and entire systems of applications, can be defined independently of infrastructure, and run in different ways (single threaded, multi-threaded, clocked, stepping, multi-process, actor model) and with different infrastructure.

links.title: dry-python
links.description: A set of libraries for pluggable business logic components.

links.title: PyAssimilator
links.description: Quickly create DDD Python patterns, Event-Based Systems, CRUD applications. Set of Python patterns for database access that support SQLAlchemy, MongoDB, Redis, or Pure Python. PyAssimilator allows you to write code without dependencies, meaning you can switch SQL to NoSQL without changing a single file in your system.

links.title: Rails Event Store
links.description: Rails Event Store (RES) is a library for publishing, consuming, storing and retrieving events. It's your best companion for going with an event-driven architecture for your Rails application.

links.title: Domain Storytelling
links.description: a knowledge-crunching technique that helps the people involved to familiarize themselves with the domain and work out a model that expresses their shared understanding. Available as a [print-out template](http://www.domainstorytelling.org/images/DST_Whiteboard-Kit.pdf), as well as open-source online tool, [WPS Modeler](https://www.wps.de/modeler) ([source](https://github.com/wps/domain-story-modeler)).
